data.title: Interactive Brokers adds Chainlink, Avalanche, Sui to crypto trading offering
data.description:
Interactive Brokers (NASDAQ:IBKR) added more cryptocurrencies to its trading platform, including Chainlink (LINK-USD), Avalanche (AVAX-USD) and Sui (SUI-USD), it said on Tuesday.

The new tokens are available to clients with an account at Zero Hash LLC, a FinCen-registered money service business and a regulated money transmitter that can operate in 51 U.S. jurisdictions

IBKR already offers trading for bitcoin (BTC-USD), Ethereum (ETH-USD), Litecoin (LTC), Bitcoin Cash (BCH-USD), Solana (SOL-USD), Cardano (ADA-USD), Ripple (XRP-USD) and Dogecoin (DOGE-USD). Customers can establish spot crypto positions and hedge their exposure with crypto futures, options and exchange-traded products.

“Recognizing the growing popularity of cryptocurrency trading, we continue to expand the number of digital assets available on our platform,” Steve Sanders, executive vice president of Marketing and Product Development at Interactive Brokers, said in a statement.

The three new tokens are available to clients with an account at Zero Hash LLC, a FinCen-registered money service business and a regulated money transmitter that can operate in 51 U.S. jurisdictions.

Crypto trading commissions at Interactive Brokers (NASDAQ:IBKR) are among the lowest in the space, it said, ranging from 0.12% to 0.18% of trade value based on monthly volume.

data.title: Centene upgraded at Jefferies on favorable Medicaid updates
data.description:
Jefferies upgraded the managed care firm Centene (NYSE:CNC) on Tuesday to Hold from Underperform, citing several favorable updates in the Medicaid market, where the company dominates.

Analyst David Windley also raised Centene’s (NYSE:CNC) price target to $64 from $53 per share, expecting the company to benefit from at least one favorable retro event in Q1 2025.

 “While a 1-time item, valuation is low enough for even this to help,” Windley wrote, arguing that Medicaid, which makes up only a small portion of CNC’s earnings per share, generates 56% of its premium revenue.

“Changing our view, conversations at AHIP's "Medicare, Medicaid, Duals & Commercial Markets Forum" suggest that Medicaid rates appear to be in-line to maybe slightly better than mgt guidance (IL retro),” the analyst wrote.

Additionally, Windley pointed to the U.S. Senate’s recently passed budget resolution, which required only $4B in federal funding cuts as opposed to nearly $600B funding cuts (mainly from Medicaid) required in the House version from healthcare alone.

“Despite reports that Senate Republicans and President Trump are committed to meaningful spending reduction, a reconciliation bill could be passed with minimal cuts to Medicaid,” Windley wrote.

With the company generating nearly 56% of its premium revenue from Medicaid, “that possibility almost certainly improves investor sentiment for Medicaid and CNC,” the analyst added.

data.title: Anglo American Platinum to apply for secondary listing in London; demerger expected May 31
data.description:
Anglo American Platinum (OTCPK:ANGPY) (OTCPK:AGPPF) said Tuesday it plans to apply for a secondary listing of its shares on the London Stock Exchange as part of its spinoff from Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY).

The South Africa-listed miner, whose main listing would remain on the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, said the London listing would benefit its geographically diverse shareholder base and assist in building its capital markets profile, enhancing the company's share trading liquidity and supporting its global shareholder base.

The move also would ensure that existing U.K. shareholders in Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY), which has a 66.7% shareholding, are not prevented from owning shares in the company following Anglo American Platinum's (OTCPK:ANGPY) (OTCPK:AGPPF) demerger, which is expected to take effect on May 31.

Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) decided to spin off its platinum business to simplify its operations following a failed takeover bid from rival BHP last year; it will continue to own a 19.9% shareholding in Anglo American Platinum (OTCPK:ANGPY) (OTCPK:AGPPF) following the demerger, as announced in February.

data.title: Wall Street's huge rally eases a little, but S&P headed for best day since November
data.description:
Wall Street's furious rebound on Tuesday came under some pressure after a U.S. government official doused hopes of any tariff exemptions in the near term. 

The three major averages quickly hit session lows after the remarks, but then recovered a little. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +2.37% at 5,182.26 points in midday trade. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +2.43% at 15,982.20 points, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +2.31% at 38,842.45 points.

The gauges opened solidly in negative territory and at one point were up nearly +4% each.

Over in the fixed-income markets, a selloff in bonds extended from Monday and pushed U.S. Treasury yields higher. According to Apollo Asset Management, the selling may be linked to the unwinding of the "basis trade." The 10-year yield (US10Y) was last up 9 basis points to 4.25%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 11 basis points to 3.83%.  

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page. 

U.S. Trade Representative ambassador Jamieson Greer said that President Trump had been clear that he won't issue any tariff exemptions any time soon. Greer's comments came in a U.S. Senate hearing. 

Earlier, hopes of a trade deal had helped shore up sentiment following a historic few days of market volatility. Trump announced his sweeping reciprocal tariffs - the steepest American trade barriers in over a century - last Wednesday. What followed was carnage as markets across the world plummeted on fears of a global trade war destroying economic growth.

The S&P 500 (SP500) cratered more than -10% over last Thursday and Friday, one of its biggest two-day declines in four decades. Investors then returned from the weekend and proceeded to whipsaw through a topsy-turvy Monday. The S&P swung from one extreme to another, gaining as much as +3.40% and also losing as much as -4.71%. The benchmark index briefly swung into bear market territory.

"The stock market briefly touched bear market territory on Monday, and for right now, we're still within the bounds of a correction and not a deeper bear market, even though this past week has been extremely uncomfortable," Clark Bellin, president and chief investment officer at Bellwether Wealth, said.

"The fact that we are starting to see some big cap tech stocks rally shows that investors are now being more discerning, rather than the 'sell everything' fear that took over the markets last week."  

"While there may be more downside ahead, we remind investors that there have been many examples of corrections that see a near 20% peak to trough decline, such as in 2018 and in 1998, only to see a steep rally soon after that is sustained for some time," he said. 

"Regardless of whether or not we see a recession, the stock market will at some point price-in the recovery. The stock market moves ahead of economic activity, and this is an important reminder for those who fear continued stock market downside if we see a recession materialize," Bellin added. 

Market participants this week will have plenty to look forward to, with upcoming economic data on inflation, several Federal Reserve speakers, and the start of the first quarter earnings season.

data.title: Elon Musk slams President Trump's top trade adviser as CEOs continue to push back on tariff effectiveness
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk has gone to battle against top Trump Administration trade adviser Peter Navarro, who is the Senior Counselor for Trade and Manufacturing and tasked with advancing Trump's trade and manufacturing agendas, including promoting protectionist policies like tariffs.

Navarro recently criticized Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) manufacturing practices, claiming that the Austin-based company is not a true car manufacturer, but merely an assembler of parts sourced internationally. Navarro noted that Tesla (TSLA) relies heavily on components from countries like Japan, China, and Taiwan, and dismissed Elon Musk's advocacy for zero tariffs between the U.S. and Europe as self-serving.

Elon Musk responded on social media site X to Navarro's comments, calling him "truly a moron" and "dumber than a sack of bricks" in a series of posts. Notably, Musk directly refuted Navarro's claims by asserting that Tesla (TSLA) produces the most American-made cars and is the most vertically integrated automotive manufacturer in the U.S. He emphasized that Tesla's vehicles are assembled domestically, primarily in California and Texas, with a high proportion of U.S.-sourced content. Musk also mocked Navarro's Harvard Ph.D., suggesting it contributed to an ego-driven mindset.

The feud is symbolic of some division within the administration and the business community on tariffs and their effectiveness. Musk has publicly opposed some tariffs, advocating free trade and zero-tariff agreements between the U.S. and Europe. However, Navarro and other officials have defended the tariffs as necessary to boost domestic manufacturing in traditional automotive hubs like Michigan and Indiana. As for the wider business world, a recent CNBC survey found that 69% of CEOs anticipate a recession due to the tariffs, and 37% plan workforce reductions.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were up 4.1% amid a broad market rally in Tuesday afternoon trading. The EV stock is down about 39% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Cosmos Health launches Sky Premium Life in Albania, secures $0.3M order
data.description:
• Cosmos Health (NASDAQ:COSM) has announced the launch of its Sky Premium Life food supplements brand in Albania, in partnership with Pharma Cell, a consumer health and medical supplement company that will serve as the distributor in the country.
  • Cosmos Health has secured an initial annual order worth $300,000 and anticipates further scaling its collaboration with Pharma Cell over time.
  • With distribution rights for several international brands, Pharma Cell has rapidly expanded its presence across the country, serving a growing network of pharmacies and healthcare professionals.
  • "Sky Premium Life continues to see strong international growth, and it’s a great pleasure to welcome our new collaboration with Pharma Cell. We look forward to working together to deliver outstanding results and establish the brand’s presence in Albania." said Greg Siokas, CEO.

data.title: Tariffs' goal is to move manufacturing back to the US: Trade Rep. Greer
data.description:
"We must move away from an economy based solely on the financial sector and government spending, and we must become an economy based on producing real goods and services," U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said during a U.S. Senate Finance Committee hearing.on Tuesday.

Greer said he has had "several" conversations with Japanese officials in the past weeks regarding trade. "We want more market access in Japan," he said. "We can have more and better agricultural market access."

"There are things we can do with our trading partners, things that aren't always purely in the trade sector with respect to economic security, whether it's export controls alignment or investment screening alignment, or energy — making sure that our partners are tied up with us with respect to liquefied natural gas and other resources as opposed to being dependent on other countries," he said.

All of these factors will come into play with the Japanese, Greer said.

As other countries approach the U.S. to seek trade deals, "what we have told them is, 'If you have a better idea to achieve reciprocity and to get our trade deficit down, we want to talk to you, we want to negotiate with you,'" he said.

Regarding the recent tariffs, President Trump "has been clear" that he won't allow exclusions or exemptions, Greer said.

During his testimony, Greer declined to estimate how much the tariffs might raise prices or even if they would raise prices at all.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Monster Beverage briefly drops after Spruce Point short report
data.description:
• Monster Beverage (NASDAQ:MNST) quickly fell to session lows, but quickly returned to positive ground after a Spruce Point short report on the energy drink maker. 
  • Spruce Point disclosed that it's short shares of Monster Beverage (NASDAQ:MNST).
  • Monster Beverage (MNST) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment.

data.title: iCAD appoints Mark Koeniguer as CCO
data.description:
• iCAD (NASDAQ:ICAD) on Tuesday announced the appointment of Mark Koeniguer as chief commercial officer (CCO), to lead the next phase of global growth for iCAD.
  • As CCO at iCAD, Koeniguer will assume responsibilities for revenue growth across North America and around the globe.
  • In his new role, Koeniguer will have oversight of the commercial sales teams, partnerships, operations and customer success, said the company.
  • Most recently, Koeniguer served as CEO of ScreenPoint Medical. He previously held executive leadership roles at Riverain Technologies, Volpara Health, R2 Technology, Stereotaxis and GE Healthcare.
  • ICAD is +1.75439% to $1.74.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cruise line stocks stage a mini-comeback after a recent sell-off due to tariff and recession concerns
data.description:
The cruise line sector broke higher on Tuesday after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said about 70 countries have approached the U.S. for tariff negotiations. "If they come to the table with solid proposals, I think we can end up with some good deals," Bessent told CNBC.

The relief rally is tied to investor hopes that a recession in the U.S. that impacts spending on travel can be avoided through a quick resolution of the tariff standoff.

There was also a notable cruise line strategy update for investors to digest this week. Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) announced on Monday that the company struck long-term charter agreements for four vessels across its three brands. The deals include purchase options.

Two Norwegian Cruise Line ships, Norwegian Sky and Norwegian Sun, will be chartered to Cordelia Cruises, a premium operator in India, starting in 2026 and 2027. Additionally, Regent Seven Seas Cruises’ Seven Seas Navigator and Oceania Cruises’ Insignia will be chartered to Crescent Seas, a residential cruise line, beginning in the same years.

The cruise line company emphasized that the agreements align with its fleet optimization strategy by repurposing vessels for markets beyond its core operations.

Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NLCH) was up 5.1% at 11:22 a.m. while Royal Caribbean (NYSE:RCL) gained 4.6% and Carnival (NYSE:CCL) was up 6.5%. Viking Holdings (NYSE:VIK) led the sector, with a rally of 7.3%.
